adds.rationale: Adds the AI-custom-silicon and AI-networking leg of the hyperscaler buildout that the existing AI cluster does not fully capture, after Broadcom's Q1 FY26 print (March 4, 2026) showed AI revenue $8.4B up 106% YoY, a $73B AI backlog, OpenAI added as the sixth named custom-silicon customer, and management guiding Q2 AI revenue to $13.1B with a 'clear line of sight' to >$100B annual AI chip revenue by late 2027/early 2028.

news_evidence.summary: Broadcom Q1 FY26 reported March 4, 2026: total revenue $19.31B (+29% YoY); AI semiconductor revenue $8.4B (+106% YoY); $73B AI-specific order backlog; Q2 AI revenue guided to $13.1B.
news_evidence.source: Broadcom investor relations / Motley Fool transcript

news_evidence.summary: Broadcom expanded named custom-silicon customer list from 5 to 6 with OpenAI joining Google, Anthropic, Meta, and two unnamed hyperscalers; management cited Google Ironwood, Anthropic scaling 1GW to 3GW, and OpenAI's first XPU in 2027 as the demand drivers behind the $73B AI backlog.
news_evidence.source: FinancialContent coverage of Q1 FY26 print

adds.rationale: Adds the HALEU fuel-cycle leg of the nuclear thesis that CCJ/CEG/SMR (uranium miner, AI-power utility, reactor designer) leave uncovered, after a $900M DOE task order to Centrus on Jan 5, 2026 to expand the Piketon, Ohio facility to commercial-scale HALEU production - the enriched uranium that next-generation SMRs and advanced reactors require and which the US has no commercial domestic supplier of today.

news_evidence.summary: On January 5, 2026, the DOE announced that American Centrifuge Operating (Centrus subsidiary) was selected for a $900M task order to expand the Piketon, Ohio uranium enrichment facility to include commercial-scale HALEU production; Centrus is planning ~12 metric tons of HALEU annual capacity for next-generation reactors.

Centrus disclosed a $2.3B commercial LEU enrichment contingent backlog and in December 2025 launched centrifuge manufacturing to support the planned Piketon HALEU expansion, positioning LEU as the only US-listed pure-play on the HALEU fuel-supply bottleneck for the SMR buildout.

removes.rationale: AI-bucket rotation at the 4-ticker per-bucket ceiling: with AVGO added, swap out TSM as the weakest direct AI exposure of the cluster (pure foundry rather than AI compute/software end-demand owner) and the one carrying the most geopolitical-tail risk; the cluster sharpens to hyperscaler-software (MSFT, ORCL), GPU (NVDA), and custom-silicon/networking (AVGO).

news_evidence.summary: Bank of America's 2026 AI-chip buy list names NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, AMD, AMAT, LRCX, CDNS, SNPS as the eight primary beneficiaries of the upgraded $1.3T 2026 semiconductor TAM; TSM appears in foundry rolls but is one step removed from AI-compute end-demand owners.
news_evidence.source: Bank of America 2026 semiconductor outlook / Yahoo Finance summary

removes.rationale: Frees a slot for the nuclear-fuel-cycle add (LEU): VRT's AI-power-infrastructure exposure is increasingly captured by the watchlist's other AI-power proxies (CEG as the AI-utility, SMR as reactor-side, the AI mega-caps directly), so the single-name power-equipment bet adds the least incremental thesis vs a focused HALEU pure-play that no other ticker in the watchlist covers.

news_evidence.summary: Q1 2026 hyperscaler-nuclear deal flow (Meta-Oklo 1.2 GW Ohio campus announced Jan 2026; Google-Kairos Hermes 2 ramp; NuScale-TVA program) is being captured directly by CEG (Constellation, the AI-utility) and SMR (NuScale, reactor designer); the AI-power thesis is well represented without VRT's equipment-vendor exposure.

rationale_overall: Two thesis-sharpening swaps at the 16-name cap. (1) AI rotation: TSM out, AVGO in - keeps AI at the 4-ticker bucket cap but rotates from pure foundry to custom-silicon/AI-networking, the leg of the Q1 FY26 hyperscaler-capex story (Broadcom $8.4B AI revenue +106% YoY, $73B backlog, OpenAI as sixth custom-silicon customer) that NVDA-plus-foundry does not fully capture. (2) Nuclear deepening: VRT out, LEU in - expands the nuclear bucket from 3 to 4 (CCJ, CEG, SMR, LEU) by adding the HALEU fuel-cycle pure-play after Centrus's Jan 5 $900M DOE task order, completing the nuclear thesis chain (uranium miner -> enrichment -> reactor designer -> AI utility); VRT's AI-power-equipment exposure is already proxied by CEG and the AI mega-caps. Post-swap shape: AI 4 (MSFT, NVDA, ORCL, AVGO), nuclear 4 (CCJ, CEG, SMR, LEU), quantum 2 (IONQ, RGTI), rockets/spacecraft 2 (RKLB, ITA), robotics 2 (ISRG, ROBO), geopolitical-energy 1 (LNG), general_markets 1 (SPY). All buckets at or under the 4-ticker diversity ceiling; watchlist remains at the 16-name cap.
